decisions.reasoning: NVDA reported Q1 FY2027 earnings on May 20 with record $81.6B revenue (85% YoY, beat $78.8B est) and EPS $1.87 (beat $1.76 est). Q2 guide of $91B well above $87.2B consensus. Blackwell GPUs described as effectively sold out. Post-earnings stock sold off ~1.5% on buy-the-rumor dynamics, dipping below our entry of $217.87, then recovering to $217.86 pre-market. Added 2 shares at market to bring position to 21 shares, using available headroom before 95% gross long cap. Bear cases noted: custom silicon competition (Google TPU, Amazon Trainium, Meta MTIA), China TAM baselined at zero, capex dependency risk if hyperscalers blink. Near-term momentum from the earnings beat and demand visibility outweighs these medium-term structural risks.

sources.outlet: Intellectia AI / NVDA Q1 FY2027 Earnings
sources.headline: Nvidia Q1 FY2027: Record $81.6B revenue, EPS $1.87 beat, Q2 guide $91B
Nvidia Earnings: Updates and Commentary May 2026
Nvidia $20T thesis intact, but bear case: custom silicon, Rubin delay, China at zero TAM

decisions.invalidation: Any Mag7 hyperscaler meaningfully cuts capex guidance on next print, or Rubin architecture delay extends beyond current expectations, or regulatory escalation further restricts H20/export products

decisions.reasoning: SMA signal is flat (fast SMA $416 vs slow SMA $417, essentially neutral). Fundamentals solid: restructured OpenAI deal (MSFT secures IP through 2032, ~$6B OpenAI revenue expected 2026), strong cloud growth in Q2 FY2026, analyst consensus target $587. Near-term headwinds include high capex and LinkedIn layoffs (600+) reflecting cost discipline. No new catalyst today to add or trim. Position at -1.70%, within normal drawdown.

sources.headline: MSFT at $417.80, market cap $3.11T; analysts target $587 consensus
Microsoft Stock MSFT in May 2026: Profit, Loss, or Stagnation?

decisions.invalidation: Azure growth decelerates below 25% in next earnings, or OpenAI cost overruns force MSFT to revise deal terms unfavorably

decisions.reasoning: SPY is our best performer (+2.44% unrealized). Long SMA signal with fast $742 well above slow $724. S&P 500 up 8.1% YTD, blended earnings growth 27.1% (FactSet May 4). Analysts raised year-end targets: Yardeni to 8,250, RBC to 7,900. Broad market tail continues. No reason to trim or add — position serves as diversified core.

sources.headline: Stock Market Live May 20, 2026: S&P 500 ticks higher ahead of Nvidia earnings
S&P 500 Forecast: Yardeni raises Wall Street target to 8,250

decisions.invalidation: FOMC turns hawkish in June meeting, or inflation prints materially above expectations causing multiple compression

decisions.reasoning: XLE up 21.6% in 2026. Brent crude at $126/barrel, WTI at $110 — geopolitical risk premium from Middle East chokepoints elevated. Energy majors (XOM and CVX = 42.5% of XLE) generating strong cash flows with structural improvements. Our entry $59.94 vs current $59.02 (-1.54%) is noise relative to the broad energy thesis. No reason to exit with oil this elevated.

sources.headline: Energy ETF XLE Soars 21.6% in 2026 as Investors Bet on Oil Majors Resilience
Oil Prices May Fall to $55 by 2026 — bear case for energy ETF noted but not base case at current $110 WTI

decisions.invalidation: WTI crude breaks below $85 on OPEC+ supply surge or demand demand destruction, or Middle East geopolitical risk premium unwinds sharply

summary: First session: added 2 NVDA shares on post-earnings beat catalyst (Q1 FY2027 record $81.6B rev, strong Q2 guide); held MSFT, SPY, XLE. Day P/L +$6.21.
open_theses: NVDA (21 shares): Blackwell GPU cycle in full swing, sold out, Blackwell ramp + inference demand = revenue visibility through FY2027; watch for hyperscaler capex signals
MSFT (5 shares): Cloud + AI monetization re-rating story; OpenAI deal restructured favorably; SMA neutral but fundamental thesis intact
SPY (3 shares): Broad market core position, long SMA, constructive macro with 27% S&P earnings growth
XLE (10 shares): Oil at $110 WTI underpins energy cash flows; geopolitical premium supports elevated prices near-term
